_about: Seed-once, read-only canonical AI block archetypes shared by ALL niches. compile.php merges these with a per-niche brief (sites/{master}/multisite/niche_brief.json) to generate that master's data/ai_block_types.json. Placeholders: [[brief.*]] are filled at COMPILE time from the niche brief; {runtime} vars ({business},{city},{state},{service}) are left intact for generate.py to substitute per-city. Do NOT use {SS} in prompts — it does not resolve; instruct Claude to use the state abbreviation instead.

_shared.guardrail: ACCURACY RULES (critical): Use only well-established general knowledge about {city}'s region, climate, and seasons. Do NOT invent or state as fact: statistics, prices, pass/success rates, review counts, years in business, awards, certifications, specific ZIP codes, specific neighborhood names, local ordinances, or any local detail you are not confident is true. If you are unsure about a specific detail for {city}, write about the broader region or state instead of inventing specifics. Never guarantee outcomes. Use the standard two-letter abbreviation for {state} where an abbreviation reads naturally. [[brief.guardrails]]
_shared.tone_line: Tone: [[brief.tone]]. Write in original wording; do not copy competitor phrasing.

city_intro.label: City Intro
city_intro.description: Standalone SEO intro (H2 + 2 paragraphs, 160-190 words) about the service in the target city.
city_intro.prompt_skeleton:
You are writing SEO content for {business}, [[brief.business_descriptor]] serving {city}, {state}. Target keyword: "{service} {city}". Write a city intro section: 1. heading_text (H2): 6-10 words, includes "{city}" and references {service} or local demand for it. 2. text: exactly 2 flowing paragraphs, 160-190 words total. - Para 1: Why [[brief.customer_noun]] in {city} need {service}, grounded in [[brief.local_angle]]. - Para 2: How {business} helps with {service} specifically — approach, thoroughness, and follow-up. Keep the focus on {service}; you may briefly note they also handle other pest problems, but do NOT list them. End forward-looking. Use "{city}" 2-3 times. No bullets, no headings inside text. Wrap each paragraph in

...

. [[shared.guardrail]] [[shared.tone_line]] LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name (pick only a few, not all): {neighborhoods} These provided names are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules above about not naming neighborhoods. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

...

. Return JSON only: {"heading_text": "...", "text": "

...

...

"}

hero_subtext.label: Hero Subtext (Inject)
hero_subtext.description: Injects 2-3 city-specific sentences into the next hero_split block's hs_subtext field.
hero_subtext.prompt_skeleton:
You are writing hero subtext for {business}, [[brief.business_descriptor]], for a page targeting {city}, {state}. Write 2-3 sentences (40-60 words total): - Open with a {city}-specific statement grounded in [[brief.local_angle]]. - Center on {service} — the specific problem {business} solves on this page. Do NOT list other pest types. - Close with a value statement for [[brief.customer_noun]]. No bullets. No exclamation marks. Professional, not sales-y. [[shared.guardrail]] [[shared.tone_line]] LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name (pick only a few, not all): {neighborhoods} These provided names are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules above about not naming neighborhoods. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

...

. Return JSON only: {"text": "2-3 sentences here"}

feature_columns_local.label: Feature Columns - Local (Standalone)
feature_columns_local.description: 4-column feature_columns block: 3 core benefits + 1 city-specific column.
feature_columns_local.prompt_skeleton:
You are writing feature columns for {business}, [[brief.business_descriptor]], for a {city}, {state} page. fc_heading: 6-10 words, city-aware and benefit-focused (e.g. "Why {city} [[brief.customer_noun]] Choose {business}"). Write exactly 4 columns: - Columns 1-3: core benefits of {business}'s {service} - things like experience, responsiveness, guarantees, thorough treatment, and trained technicians. Do NOT list other pest types. - Column 4: a {city}-specific benefit grounded in [[brief.local_angle]]. Each column: heading 3-5 words (benefit-focused); text 20-30 words, concrete. [[shared.guardrail]] [[shared.tone_line]] LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name (pick only a few, not all): {neighborhoods} These provided names are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules above about not naming neighborhoods. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

...

. Return JSON only: {"fc_heading": "...", "columns": [{"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}]}

faq_local.label: FAQ - Local (Inject)
faq_local.description: Appends 2 city-specific FAQ items to the next faq_two_col block's fq_items array.
faq_local.prompt_skeleton:
You are writing FAQ content for {business}, [[brief.business_descriptor]], for a {city}, {state} page about {service}. Write 6 FAQ items a {city} [[brief.customer_noun]] would realistically search for. Every question AND answer must be about {service} specifically — do NOT ask about unrelated services or other service types. Cover a MIX of topics so the set is not repetitive: (1) what {service} involves or what to expect, (2) how quickly {business} can help in {city}, (3) cost or pricing factors for {service}, (4) how to prepare for or what happens during {service}, (5) prevention or keeping the problem from happening again, and (6) local seasonal timing or regional factors for {service} in this area ([[brief.local_angle]]). Do not fabricate prices, guarantees, timelines, or credentials. Each item: - question: specific and searchable (8-15 words); at least half naturally include "{city}". - answer: 40-70 words, grounded in real regional context, wrapped in

...

. [[shared.guardrail]] [[shared.tone_line]] LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name (pick only a few, not all): {neighborhoods} These provided names are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules above about not naming neighborhoods. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

...

. Return JSON only: {"items": [{"question": "...", "answer": "..."}, ... 6 items total]}

seasonal_calendar.label: Seasonal Calendar (Standalone)
seasonal_calendar.description: 4-step seasonal breakdown of pressure/demand by season for the region. Opt-in for seasonal niches (pest, wildlife, remediation).
seasonal_calendar.prompt_skeleton:
You are writing a seasonal guide for {business}, [[brief.business_descriptor]], for {city}, {state}. steps_heading: 5-9 words referencing seasons in {city} and {service}. Write exactly 4 items, one per season (Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter): - heading: the season plus a 2-4 word theme. - text: 25-40 words on what [[brief.customer_noun]] in {city}'s region typically face that season, grounded in [[brief.local_angle]], and how {business} helps. Base seasonal patterns on the region's real climate. Do not invent specific data. [[shared.guardrail]] [[shared.tone_line]] LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name (pick only a few, not all): {neighborhoods} These provided names are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules above about not naming neighborhoods. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

...

. Return JSON only: {"steps_heading": "...", "steps_items": [{"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}, {"heading": "...", "text": "..."}]}

local_relevance.label: Local Relevance / Neighborhood-type (Standalone)
local_relevance.description: Standalone text on local property/area characteristics. Generic-safe by default (no invented place names); upgrades to named neighborhoods only when the enrichment step supplies verified names.
local_relevance.prompt_skeleton:
You are writing a local-relevance section for {business}, [[brief.business_descriptor]], for {city}, {state}. heading_text (H2): 5-9 words referencing serving {city} and the surrounding area. text: 1-2 paragraphs (110-150 words) on how property characteristics across {city} affect {service} - e.g. differences between older, established neighborhoods (mature trees, aging construction) and newer subdivisions - grounded in [[brief.local_angle]]. Whether to name specific neighborhoods is governed by the LOCAL AREAS line below. [[shared.guardrail]] [[shared.tone_line]] LOCAL AREAS - the real, verified {city} neighborhoods you ARE permitted to name (pick only a few, not all): {neighborhoods} These provided names are the ONE exception to the accuracy rules above about not naming neighborhoods. If that line lists actual names, you MUST choose ONLY 2 OR 3 of them - never 4 or more, even though more are listed - and name those BY NAME as natural in-sentence examples - always woven into prose, NEVER a bulleted or "we serve X, Y, Z" list, and never add any name not on that line. If that line is blank or still shows the literal token {neighborhoods}, name NO specific neighborhoods, streets, or ZIP codes and speak only in general terms about area types. Wrap paragraphs in

...

. Return JSON only: {"heading_text": "...", "text": "

...

"}
